commands.review_summary:
The PR threads the existing `agents.defaults.timeFormat` value through the Control UI bootstrap payload and applies it to shared Control UI/WebChat timestamp formatting helpers.

PR surface: Source +32, Tests +102. Total +134 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main exposes `agents.defaults.timeFormat`, but the Control UI/WebChat timestamp path has no `ui/src` consumer and formats via browser locale defaults only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No code repair is indicated from this review; the next action is contributor real behavior proof, maintainer-supplied proof, or a maintainer proof override before merge.

target.title: fix(control-ui): respect agents.defaults.timeFormat for timestamps
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes the Control UI / WebChat rendering timestamps with a hardcoded locale clock, ignoring the existing `agents.defaults.timeFormat` setting (e.g. showing `7:17`/`3:30 AM` when the operator configured 24‑hour).
- Threads the resolved `agents.defaults.timeFormat` through the gateway bootstrap config into a small UI hour‑cycle helper, so `formatMs` / `formatTimeMs` / `formatDateTimeMs` and the WebChat group timestamp all honor `12` / `24`.
- Leaves `auto` (and unset) as the browser locale default, so existing deployments render exactly as before — only an explicit `12`/`24` forces an hour cycle.

## Linked context

Closes #58147

The config key already exists (`src/config/types.agent-defaults.ts`, `agents.defaults.timeFormat: "auto" | "12" | "24"`) and is documented as controlling 12h/24h rendering; the Control UI was simply not wired to it. No new config surface is added.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Control UI timestamps respect `agents.defaults.timeFormat`.
- Real environment tested: real Chromium (Playwright) loading the actual Vite‑served production UI modules (`ui/src/ui/format.ts`, `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts`) via the Control UI E2E server, plus the real gateway bootstrap HTTP handler.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Booted the Control UI E2E Vite server, opened it in Chromium, then called the production `setUiTimeFormatPreference(...)` + `formatChatTimestampForDisplay(...)` / `formatTimeMs(...)` for a fixed timestamp (`2026-01-15T19:30:00Z`) at `12` / `24` / `auto`, and screenshotted the rendered `.chat-group-timestamp` elements.
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts` exercises the real `handleControlUiHttpRequest` emitting `timeFormat` from `agents.defaults.timeFormat`.
- Evidence after fix (real browser output):
  - `timeFormat=24` → time `19:30`, group label `Jan 16, 2026, 03:30` (no AM/PM)
  - `timeFormat=12` → time `07:30 PM`, group label `Jan 16, 2026, 3:30 AM`
  - `timeFormat=auto` → group label `Jan 16, 2026, 3:30 AM` (browser default, unchanged)
- Observed result after fix: the rendered timestamp flips between 24-hour and 12-hour exactly as configured; `auto` matches the prior locale-default rendering.
- What was not tested: a full end-to-end click-through of a live gateway session in the browser (used the E2E server + mock bootstrap); per-agent timeFormat overrides are out of scope — this fix consumes the global `agents.defaults` value the issue names.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: full `pnpm build` could not complete in this WSL checkout (resource limits / SIGKILL); type lanes (`tsgo:core`, `tsgo:test:ui`, `tsgo:test:src`) and the focused test suites pass locally, and CI covers the full build.

## Tests and validation

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm format:check` (changed files clean)
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs` lanes: `tsgo:core`, `tsgo:test:ui`, `tsgo:test:src` — green
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/format.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/control-ui-bootstrap.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts`

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes` — Control UI timestamps now follow `agents.defaults.timeFormat`; `auto`/unset is unchanged from today.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No` — reuses the existing `agents.defaults.timeFormat` key; the bootstrap payload field is additive and optional.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`.

Highest-risk area: the centralized hour-cycle merge inside the shared `format.ts` helpers affects every Control UI timestamp surface (cron, usage, activity, chat).

How is that risk mitigated? `auto`/unset resolves to an empty options object, so behavior is byte-identical to current rendering unless an operator explicitly sets `12`/`24`; caller-supplied options still override the resolved cycle.

Current review state: maintainer review and CI.

AI-assisted (Claude Code). Proof above was run manually in a real browser.
target.author: zengwen-dt
